banked_reason: Stage 0.5 (oneshot field-tier review, 2026-05-14T15:55:41Z) returned status=revise / classification=novelty: derivation collapses to standard finite response-type sharpness + baseline-pooling witness; T<=2 minimality conjecture refuted under stated coarsening. Mathematically sound (correctness=pass) but below novelty_target=field.

proposed_from.topic: Partial identification of dynamic treatment effects under non-Markov assignments and imperfect compliance
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains ThmSmith doc/API.md section 3 plus pid_late_bounded_defiers, which covers a one-period CLATE/bounded-defier partial-ID envelope, and Identification/ExactID/DynamicLATE, which is a two-period exact-ID result under stronger assumptions. The revised proposal still differs by targeting a T=3 full-history dynamic IV partial-ID LP, a mean-valued local-certificate nonpasteability conjecture, and strict loss from Markov or stagewise compression.","published_axis":"Closest published competitors are BalkePearl1997 for static binary-IV sharp bounds, DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 for generic automated sharp bounds in discrete causal settings, ChenZhang2023 and Han2024 for finite-horizon dynamic-IV/partial-welfare LP frameworks, and SwansonLabrecqueHernan2018 for sustained-treatment IV null tests. The epsilon-gap is an analytic theorem-level history-separation/no-pasting phenomenon for one joint dynamic compliance response map, plus a T<=2 no-separation boundary not stated by those papers."}
seed_list: Prior work ChenZhang2023 would imply that time-varying IV partial-identification can be organized through Bellman-style dynamic-regime objects. Our seed conjectures that Bellman or stagewise intervals are nonsharp under non-Markov assignment because one latent compliance response map must be pasted across histories.

Prior work Han2024 establishes sharp partial welfare orderings via LPs over dynamic regimes. Our seed conjectures that a named sustained-regime effect can be sign-bounded even when the welfare order is unresolved, because non-Markov assignment separates compliance histories that regime-level orderings pool.

Prior work BalkePearl1997 establishes tight static bounds for imperfect compliance. Our seed conjectures that adaptive dynamic IVs create new cross-history facets absent from a Cartesian product of static Balke-Pearl polytopes.

Prior work XuZhuShiLuoSong2023 would treat Markov structure as the natural sequential IV/OPE state space. Our seed conjectures that non-Markov behavior-policy labels are themselves identifying information and can strictly tighten partial-ID bounds.

Prior work ArtmanBhattacharyaErtefaieLynchMcKayJohnson2024 models partial compliance in SMARTs with marginal structural models. Our seed conjectures that a nonparametric full-history bound can reverse the sign suggested by marginal compliance summaries because compliance-history principal strata are coupled.

Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 uses mutual consistency across multiple IV choice models. Our seed conjectures that history-specific encouragement nodes create a dynamic mutual-consistency restriction across one compliance response map that strictly tightens policy-effect bounds.

Prior work GabrielSjolanderSachs2023 establishes sharp bounds for imperfect randomized experiments. Our seed conjectures that sequential randomized encouragement with imperfect compliance has sharper bounds than per-stage no-defier analyses because treatment paths share latent response types.

Prior work PuZhang2021 defines IV-optimal single-stage rules under partial identification. Our seed conjectures that dynamic policy-improvement intervals can fail to paste across histories and can reverse when global latent compliance compatibility is enforced.

Prior work CuiTchetgen2021 gives point identification of optimal regimes under stronger IV conditions. Our seed conjectures that without those no-interaction conditions, adaptive non-Markov assignment can still sign-identify a sustained effect through full-history LP constraints.

Prior work ManskiPepper2000 shows monotone IV restrictions can be weak alone. Our seed conjectures that non-Markov randomized encouragement can create endogenous ordering information across histories, yielding MIV-like tightening without imposing a new shape restriction.

Prior work HeckmanHumphriesVeramendi2016 warns that dynamic IV estimates lack policy interpretation unless instruments are policy variables. Our seed conjectures that, when the instruments are policy encouragements but compliance is imperfect, full-history bounds recover a policy-relevant sustained-regime target.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChenZhang2023 would imply that time-varying IV partial-identification can be organized through Bellman-style dynamic-regime objects. Our seed conjectures that Bellman or stagewise intervals are nonsharp under non-Markov assignment because one latent compliance response map must be pasted across histories.

anchor_paper.author: Chen and Zhang
anchor_paper.claim: Derives IV-optimal DTRs and a Bellman equation under partial identification with a time-varying IV.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the claim is not iid-to-Markov or Markov-to-non-Markov extension; it predicts a sharpness failure from cross-history latent-map compatibility.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChenZhang2023 because their partial-ID Bellman equation already handles histories.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their object is IV-optimality/policy improvement, not a proof that local Bellman intervals equal the sharp latent-table interval for a named sustained treatment-path effect under non-Markov assignment.
seed_details.burned_reason: Pursued as angle 0 (5 revisions of proposal, 2 oneshot derivations). Stage 0.5 field-tier review (2026-05-14T15:55:41Z) judged the full-history-vs-Bellman LP setup collapses to standard finite response-type sharpness + baseline-pooling coarsening witness, with the proposed T<=2 minimality boundary refuted at T=1 under the stated coarsening. Future runs on this anchor topic should skip seed 0 unless re-stated as a post-baseline full-history separation theorem retaining assignment-relevant baseline variables.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Han2024 establishes sharp partial welfare orderings via LPs over dynamic regimes. Our seed conjectures that a named sustained-regime effect can be sign-bounded even when the welfare order is unresolved, because non-Markov assignment separates compliance histories that regime-level orderings pool.

anchor_paper.author: Han
anchor_paper.claim: Characterizes optimal dynamic regimes by sharp partial welfare ordering via linear programs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target-variation pattern, not blocked: the target switch reveals an identification gap hidden by regime-ordering summaries.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Han2024 because welfare gaps are dynamic treatment effects.
seed_details.rebuttal: Han's LP orders regime welfares; it does not isolate the history-compression loss for a sustained path effect under one-sided dynamic compliance.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BalkePearl1997 establishes tight static bounds for imperfect compliance. Our seed conjectures that adaptive dynamic IVs create new cross-history facets absent from a Cartesian product of static Balke-Pearl polytopes.

anchor_paper.author: Balke and Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Gives tight nonparametric formulas for static binary-IV treatment-effect bounds under imperfect compliance.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method from static to dynamic, not blocked: the proposed new term is a cross-history facet and failure of product-pasting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate by applying Balke-Pearl separately at each history.
seed_details.rebuttal: Separate static tables need not be compatible with a single dynamic response map, which is exactly the conjectured obstruction.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work XuZhuShiLuoSong2023 would treat Markov structure as the natural sequential IV/OPE state space. Our seed conjectures that non-Markov behavior-policy labels are themselves identifying information and can strictly tighten partial-ID bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Xu, Zhu, Shi, Luo, and Song
anchor_paper.claim: Develops IV off-policy evaluation in confounded Markov decision processes.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Markov-to-non-Markov pattern, not blocked: the mechanism is a strict bound-tightening/sign phenomenon, not a routine state-space expansion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from using history as the state in an MDP.
seed_details.rebuttal: Using history as state does not by itself prove sharp partial-ID tightening relative to Markov compression under imperfect compliance.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ArtmanBhattacharyaErtefaieLynchMcKayJohnson2024 models partial compliance in SMARTs with marginal structural models. Our seed conjectures that a nonparametric full-history bound can reverse the sign suggested by marginal compliance summaries because compliance-history principal strata are coupled.

anchor_paper.author: Artman et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Develops a marginal structural model for partial compliance in SMARTs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; this is a nonparametric-bounds versus MSM-summary phenomenon.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is applied modeling rather than a theorem-level novelty.
seed_details.rebuttal: The theorem kernel is a sharp finite LP and sign-reversal certificate, not a new SMART estimator.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 uses mutual consistency across multiple IV choice models. Our seed conjectures that history-specific encouragement nodes create a dynamic mutual-consistency restriction across one compliance response map that strictly tightens policy-effect bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Mogstad, Torgovitsky, and Walters
anchor_paper.claim: Develops policy evaluation with multiple IVs using partial monotonicity and mutual consistency.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition pattern, not blocked: the mechanism is dynamic mutual consistency across histories, not adding another IV to the same model.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already multiple-IV mutual consistency in MTW2024.
seed_details.rebuttal: MTW targets static/policy-evaluation MTE-style choice models, not sequential noncompliance response maps with adaptive assignment likelihoods.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GabrielSjolanderSachs2023 establishes sharp bounds for imperfect randomized experiments. Our seed conjectures that sequential randomized encouragement with imperfect compliance has sharper bounds than per-stage no-defier analyses because treatment paths share latent response types.

anchor_paper.author: Gabriel, Sjolander, and Sachs
anchor_paper.claim: Derives nonparametric bounds for imperfect randomized experiments with noncompliance and missingness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends static experiment to sequential setting, not blocked: the extension predicts strict tightening from shared path-level response types.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Balke-Pearl/Gabriel bounds with more variables.
seed_details.rebuttal: The novelty is not variable count but the cross-history equality constraints induced by a single dynamic compliance map.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work PuZhang2021 defines IV-optimal single-stage rules under partial identification. Our seed conjectures that dynamic policy-improvement intervals can fail to paste across histories and can reverse when global latent compliance compatibility is enforced.

anchor_paper.author: Pu and Zhang
anchor_paper.claim: Defines IV-optimal individualized rules under partial identification in the single-stage setting.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends single-stage to dynamic, not blocked: the proposed phenomenon is non-pasting and reversal under global compatibility.
seed_details.referee_objection: Dynamic extension is handled by ChenZhang2023.
seed_details.rebuttal: Chen-Zhang handles IV-optimal DTR estimation; the seed asks a sharpness question about latent-table compatibility for fixed path effects.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CuiTchetgen2021 gives point identification of optimal regimes under stronger IV conditions. Our seed conjectures that without those no-interaction conditions, adaptive non-Markov assignment can still sign-identify a sustained effect through full-history LP constraints.

anchor_paper.author: Cui and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies value functions and optimal regimes with a binary IV under endogeneity using stronger identifying conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens assumption pattern, not blocked: the weakening is paired with a sign-identification phase phenomenon from full-history constraints.
seed_details.referee_objection: Without the point-ID assumptions this is just wide Manski bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The conjecture asserts a sign-relevant lower bound in a finite adaptive design, which is a falsifiable LP certificate rather than a generic weakening.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ManskiPepper2000 shows monotone IV restrictions can be weak alone. Our seed conjectures that non-Markov randomized encouragement can create endogenous ordering information across histories, yielding MIV-like tightening without imposing a new shape restriction.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces MIV assumptions and studies their identifying power alone and with MTR/MTS.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; the mechanism is design-induced history ordering rather than an imposed shape restriction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just an MIV assumption in disguise.
seed_details.rebuttal: The ordering comes from known randomized assignment probabilities and response-map compatibility, not from assumed monotonicity of potential outcomes in an instrument.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HeckmanHumphriesVeramendi2016 warns that dynamic IV estimates lack policy interpretation unless instruments are policy variables. Our seed conjectures that, when the instruments are policy encouragements but compliance is imperfect, full-history bounds recover a policy-relevant sustained-regime target.

anchor_paper.author: Heckman, Humphries, and Veramendi
anchor_paper.claim: Develops dynamic treatment effects and shows IV may not estimate interpretable dynamic parameters unless policy variables serve as instruments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — phenomenon is partial-ID recovery in the policy-instrument case, not a routine target relabeling.
seed_details.referee_objection: Their policy-variable IV framework already covers this case.
seed_details.rebuttal: They focus on model-based dynamic treatment effects and IV interpretation, not sharp nonparametric bounds under imperfect compliance.

proposed_from.literature_map: Balke and Pearl 1997, JASA, BalkePearl1997: tight static binary-IV bounds for imperfect compliance; tension: no dynamic compliance response map. Manski and Pepper 2000, Econometrica, ManskiPepper2000: MIV weakens classical IV; tension: credible restrictions often have limited power unless combined. Swanson et al. 2018, JASA, SwansonHernanMillerRobinsRichardson2018: surveys binary-IV partial-ID methods; open gap: dynamic treatment-path effects. Gabriel, Sjolander, and Sachs 2023, JASA, GabrielSjolanderSachs2023: sharp nonparametric bounds for imperfect randomized experiments; gap: sequential treatment paths. Heckman, Humphries, and Veramendi 2016, Journal of Econometrics, HeckmanHumphriesVeramendi2016: dynamic IV estimators may lack policy interpretation unless instruments are policy variables; gap: partial-ID policy-variable case with noncompliance. Pu and Zhang 2021, JRSSB, PuZhang2021: IV-optimal single-stage rules under partial ID; gap: global dynamic response-map compatibility. Cui and Tchetgen Tchetgen 2021, JASA, CuiTchetgen2021: point-ID optimal regimes under binary IV and no-interaction-style restrictions; tension: weaker assumptions need intervals. Chen and Zhang 2023, JRSSB, ChenZhang2023: IV-optimal DTRs and Bellman equation under partial ID; open tension: Bellman/local intervals versus one joint latent compliance table. Han 2024, JASA, Han2024: sharp partial welfare ordering for dynamic regimes via LP; gap: named sustained-regime effect and history-compression loss. Xu et al. 2023, ICML, XuZhuShiLuoSong2023: IV off-policy evaluation in Markov decision processes; tension: non-Markov assignment may be signal, not nuisance. Artman et al. 2024, Annals of Applied Statistics, ArtmanBhattacharyaErtefaieLynchMcKayJohnson2024: partial compliance in SMARTs; gap: nonparametric sharp bounds. Mogstad, Torgovitsky, and Walters 2024, Journal of Econometrics, MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024: mutual consistency across multiple IV models; phenomenon: dynamic histories may impose analogous cross-history consistency.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Prior work ChenZhang2023 would imply that time-varying IV partial-identification can be organized through Bellman-style dynamic-regime objects. Our seed conjectures that Bellman or stagewise intervals are nonsharp under non-Markov assignment because one latent compliance response map must be pasted across histories.
seeds_burned.reason: Pursued as angle 0 (5 revisions of proposal, 2 oneshot derivations). Stage 0.5 field-tier review (2026-05-14T15:55:41Z) judged the full-history-vs-Bellman LP setup collapses to standard finite response-type sharpness + baseline-pooling coarsening witness, with the proposed T<=2 minimality boundary refuted at T=1 under the stated coarsening. Future runs on this anchor topic should skip seed 0 unless re-stated as a post-baseline full-history separation theorem retaining assignment-relevant baseline variables.
